item.title: ISD holds iftar for partner organisations’ children
item.description: The student-led iftar programme returned to International School Dhaka as the school hosted its annual initiative on its premises in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Eminent journalist ABM Musa’s 95th birth anniversary today
item.description: Today is the 95th birth anniversary of eminent journalist and columnist ABM Musa....

item.title: Local government elections will be held on time: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Friday said that local government elections would be held on time....

item.title: 10 injured in Jhenaidah BNP factional clash
item.description: AT LEAST 10 people, including two police constables, were injured in a factional clash between two groups of Bangladesh Nationalist Party during a post-election meeting at Kaliganj upazila in Jhenaidah on Friday....

item.title: Children’s hour draws crowd, many stalls not fully ready
item.description: A large number of visitors thronged the Amar Ekushey Book Fair during Shishu Prahar (children’s hour) in the morning on Friday, a weekly holiday, but construction works at many stalls were going on at the fairground in the capital Dhaka on the second day of the fair....

item.title: Hillary quizzed on Epstein, urges Trump to testify
item.description: Hillary Clinton used her forced appearance Thursday before a Republican-led panel probing Jeffrey Epstein to go on the offensive and demand president Donald Trump testify about his own links to the sex offender....

item.title: Experts for effective model for 7 colleges
item.description: The government’s recent decision to review the Dhaka Central University Ordinance, 2026, involving seven government colleges in Dhaka city is an opportunity to take effective steps to improve quality of education in these colleges, observe academics....

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 5 in Gaza
item.description: Gaza’s civil defence agency said Israeli strikes killed at least five people on Friday, the latest violence despite a US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas....

item.title: US allows staff to leave Israel as Trump threatens Iran
item.description: The United States authorised the departure of non-emergency embassy staff from Israel on Friday, as it threatened strikes on Iran and pressed its biggest military build-up in the Middle East in decades....

item.title: Charges pressed against Hasina, 25 others
item.description: The police have submitted a charge sheet against ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina and 25 others in the case filed over the killing of 24-year-old Abuzar Sheikh in the capital’s Gulshan area during the 2024 July mass uprising....

item.title: 329 killed as Pakistan, Afghanistan lock in war
item.description: Pakistan bombed major cities in Afghanistan including the capital Kabul on Friday, with Islamabad’s defence minister declaring the neighbours at ‘open war’ following months of tit-for-tat clashes....

item.title: External loan-driven projects pose heightened challenges
item.description: Several multi-billion dollar projects initiated mostly with foreign loans in the past decade have yet to be completed despite raising costs and extending deadline on a number of occasions, posing challenges to the new government....

item.title: URDU IN BANGLADESH: Between memory and margins
item.description: THE evolution of Urdu was not confined to a single region. It was born in Shahjahanabad, the present-day Delhi. It spent its formative years in Lucknow and its surrounding areas, came of age in the Deccan and gradually spread across the Indian......

item.title: Trade and business challenges for new government
item.description: THE parliamentary election in Bangladesh on February 12, 2026, marked a dramatic political shift. This transition has ended the long period of political uncertainty since the ousting of the previous government in 2024. It has been met with cautious optimism among......

item.title: Faces of America’s capitalism
item.description: AMERICA’S economic model has two faces: one of domestic care and another of global extraction. Understanding this duality is essential to making sense of both its global influence and the resentment it provokes. Capitalism may appear singular in name, but......

item.title: Transforming medical education
item.description: IN FEBRUARY 2026, Bangladesh witnessed a free and fair national election, resulting in the formation of a new government under prime minister Tarique Rahman. The BNP-led alliance assumed office with clear commitments in its manifesto: welfare-oriented......

item.title: Can states stay in control?
item.description: SECURITY and sovereignty have for centuries been anchored in geography. States exercise authority within clearly defined territorial boundaries, enforce laws through physical institutions, control critical infrastructure and protect citizens using......

item.title: Loan waiver brings relief, reform should follow
item.description: THE decision to waive agricultural loans of up to Tk 10,000, including accrued interest, is a commendable step of new government towards easing burdens of smallholder and marginal farmers. Announced at the first cabinet meeting, the measure......

item.title: Award announced, honour denied
item.description: WHAT has happened centring on poet Mohon Raihan being named as the recipient, along with eight others, of the Bangla Academy Literary Award 2025 and then being denied the award on the day of the award handover is an indecent episode. The Bangla Academy......

item.title: Australian supermarket giant reins in AI assistant claiming to be human
item.description: Australian supermarket giant Woolworths has been forced to rein in an AI-powered customer service assistant after users reported it had been rambling about its mother.......Breaking news alerts

item.title: EU to provisionally apply contested South America trade deal
item.description: The European Union will implement a mammoth trade deal with the South American Mercosur bloc while waiting for a top court’s ruling on its legality, EU chief Ursula von der Leyen said Friday....

item.title: Paramount poised to acquire Warner Bros after Netflix walks away
item.description: Paramount Skydance, run by pro-Trump technology heir David Ellison, was poised to take control of Warner Bros Discovery on Thursday after Netflix said it would not raise its takeover offer, ending one of the biggest media bidding wars in a generation....

item.title: Tech sovereignty push to meet AI fever at Mobile World Congress
item.description: The giant Mobile World Congress trade fair for telecoms kicks off for its 20th annual edition in Barcelona on Monday, with firms racing to pump AI into their systems even as debate rages over the technology’s regulation....

item.title: Good weather sparks hopes of bumper litchi harvest
item.description: The beautiful flowering of litchi trees in a favourable climate has encouraged farmers and raised hopes of a bumper harvest of the juicy fruit in the Rangpur agricultural region this season....Top news summaries

item.title: Ecuador approves mining law in area of Galapagos
item.description: Ecuador’s parliament approved a mining law on Thursday criticised by environmentalists for allowing extraction in an area of the Galapagos Islands, a famed archipelago with unique flora and fauna....

item.title: US tariffs hit less than expected: European development bank
item.description: The economic impact of US president Donald Trump’s tariffs was ‘much lower’ than expected last year, Europe’s development bank said Thursday, as it raised its growth forecast for 2026....

item.title: Booming markets propel HKEx profits to record high
item.description: Hong Kong’s stock exchange reported record profits for the second consecutive year on Thursday, after the finance hub topped the global rankings for initial public offerings in 2025....

item.title: US eases Cuba oil embargo but demands ‘dramatic’ change
item.description: The United States on Wednesday eased an oil embargo on Cuba but secretary of state Marco Rubio called for the island to change ‘dramatically,’ saying the communist government had only itself to blame for a historic economic crisis....

item.title: What’s the state of Trump’s tariffs?
item.description: US president Donald Trump’s push to reimpose tariffs after his Supreme Court setback has raised questions about his trade deals and future plans, while fuelling a rush for refunds....

item.title: Automaker Stellantis posts massive loss, pivots from EV
item.description: Troubled automaker Stellantis, behind brands like Jeep and Fiat, announced Thursday a net loss of 22.3 billion euros ($26.3 billion) for last year, blaming a lack of demand for electric vehicles....

item.title: Export ban sparks rush to process lithium in Zimbabwe
item.description: Zimbabwe’s ban on raw lithium exports is forcing Chinese miners to rethink their strategy, speeding up plans to process the metal locally instead of shipping it to China’s vast rechargeable battery industry....

item.title: Kashadaha Canal re-excavation inaugurated in Manikganj
item.description: The re-excavation work of the Kashadaha Canal, which bears the memory of the late president Ziaur Rahman, has been inaugurated under the initiative of the Water Management Cooperative Society at Shibalay in Manikganj....

item.description: At least nine people were injured in a clash between the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the Jamaat-e-Islami activists at Bhatpara Bazar of Baghtia under Abhaynagar in Jashore on Thursday....

item.title: DMP files 1,493 cases for violating traffic rules
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police’s traffic fivision has filed 1,493 cases across the capital for violating traffic rules....

item.title: Adequate TCB stocks will keep market stable: state minister Alam
item.description: State minister for commerce Md Shariful Alam on Friday said that the government had maintained adequate stocks ofTrading Corporation of Bangladesh products to keep the commodity market stable....

item.title: Law minister wants to establised judiciary envisioned by all
item.description: Law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Friday said that he wanted to move forward along the path of the judiciary envisioned by all....

item.title: ASI among 3 killed in road accidents
item.description: At least three people, including an assistant sub-inspector of police, were killed in road accidents in Sylhet, Chattogram and Gazipur on Friday....

item.title: Eight shops burnt at Kuakata
item.description: Eight shops were burnt in a fire at the Kuakata tourist town of Kalapara upazlia in Patuakhali district early Friday morning....

item.title: Barishal city official sent to jail over rape charge
item.description: The police have arrested Barishal City Corporation official Shahidul Islam Ujjal, 40, on charges of raping a housewife and recording a video of the act on the pretext of marriage....

item.title: DWEAB demands exemplary punishment
item.description: The Domestic Workers Employed Association of Bangladesh on Friday protested at the abuse of a child domestic worker at Ashulia of Savar in Dhaka....

item.title: Farmers in Thakurgaon count losses as potato prices fall
item.description: Farmers in Thakurgaon, a major potato-producing district in northern Bangladesh, are facing severe financial distress as potato prices have plummeted in local markets....

item.title: Govt plans one-stop land services to end harassment: land minister
item.description: Land minister Mizanur Rahman Minu on Friday said that the government was working to introduce a one-stop land service centre to reduce public harassment and streamline land-related services....

item.title: Son sets father’s house on fire in Kushtia
item.description: A dwelling house was gutted by fire after a drug-addicted son of the family who lived there allegedly set it on fire at Daulatpur in Kushtia on Friday....

item.title: Govt works for public welfare: state minister Tuku
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock, agriculture and food Sultan Salahuddin Tuku on Friday said that under the leadership of prime minister Tarique Rahman, they were working with utmost sincerity from their respective positions for the welfare of the people....

item.title: Forest dept has 1.5cr saplings for planting this year
item.description: Against the government’s target to plant 25 crore trees over the next five years, or five crore annually, the Forest Department currently has about 1.5 crore saplings available for this year’s plantation drive....

item.description: Secretary general of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Friday said thjat the BNP would move forward being inspired with the principles of 1952, ‘71, ‘90 and 2024....

item.title: France warns of ‘provocation’ over Russian drone
item.description: France’s foreign minister said Friday that if a drone seen this week near a French aircraft carrier visiting Sweden turned out to be Russian—a claim Moscow called ‘absurd’—it would be a ‘ridiculous provocation’....

item.title: Factory worker stabbed to death in Chattogram
item.description: A factory worker was stabbed to death in Chattogram city on Thursday night....

item.description: Nepal’s former police chief ordered the use of lethal force during the September 2025 anti-corruption protests, a BBC investigation revealed....

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman is scheduled to inaugurate the distribution of Family Cards on March 10 in Bogura Sadar Upazila....

item.title: Power supply to remain suspended in parts of Sylhet Saturday
item.description: Power supply will remain suspended for nine hours in several areas of Sylhet on Saturday due to annual emergency repair, maintenance work and tree pruning....

item.title: Youth confesses to Hazaribagh schoolgirl murder
item.description: A Dhaka court on Friday recorded the confessional statement of a youth in a case filed over a school girl being stabbed to death in Dhaka city’s Hazaribagh area....

item.description: A three-day printmaking workshop titled Impressions of Rokeya–2, conducted by eminent artist Professor Rokeya Sultana, began at Cosmos-Atelier71 at Cosmos Centre in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: Bus catches fire after collision with motorcycle in Gazipur
item.description: A passenger bus caught fire after colliding with a motorbike in the Board Bazar area of Gazipur on Friday....

item.description: A conversational session was organised on Friday centring Shankor Sawjal’s photography at Alliance Française de Dhaka, the French cultural centre in the capital....

item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard has conducted special operation to eliminate robbers and pirates in the maritime areas of Maheskhali and Kutubdia in Cox’s Bazar....

item.title: Moringa blossoms spread serene white beauty across Rajshahi city
item.description: Snow-white blossoms of moringa tree, locally known as sajney, are spreading a sense of serenity and charm across Rajshahi city....

item.title: Labour leaders want wage, allowance by Ramadan 20
item.description: Leaders of two garment workers’ rights bodies at separate rallies in Dhaka on Friday called on the government and garment factory owners to pay workers’ wages and festival allowances by Ramadan 20....
